top_posts.text:
4 lessons from running a Threads account that writes itself:

1. The official Meta API is free — no browser hacks, zero ban risk.
2. GitHub Actions + free AI APIs = $0/month forever.
3. A daily feedback loop (post → analytics → better post) compounds faster than any strategy.
4. Tokens auto-refresh. I haven’t logged in since setup day.

Two hours one Saturday. That’s the entire investment.

Stop manually scheduling posts. Instead, create a content loop: let your performance data dictate tomorrow's topic. I automated this with free tools and the official API. Now my account writes its own posts based on what actually works. No guesswork, no burnout.
I built a Threads account that writes, posts, and improves without me touching it. It uses the official Meta API. Free tools. One-time setup. And yes, you’re reading a post it wrote right now.

Consistency isn't about willpower. It's about architecture.

Set up a feedback loop once: posts generate analytics, analytics inform tomorrow's content, AI rewrites it better. Official Meta API. Free tools. Two hours.

The system improves itself. You just watch.

Most creators waste hours manually posting every day. Instead, build a feedback loop:  
Post → Read analytics → Let AI rewrite tomorrow’s content based on what worked.  

I set this up once with free tools (GitHub Actions + free AI APIs). It now runs itself. No daily grind.  

That’s the whole trick. One setup, zero upkeep.
